title: Immunohistology to Confirm ChR2 Expression in Mouse Brain Slices
author: Mai-Anh Vu

steps.title: In preparation for immunohistology, Lugol’s solution...
steps.instruction: In preparation for immunohistology, Lugol’s solution was removed from brain tissue after CT imaging by soaking the implanted brains in a solution of 5% sodium thiosulfate (STS) (see Materials ) in 1% PBS for 4-6 days ( Hopkins et al. , 2015 ).

steps.title: The implant was then removed, the...
steps.instruction: The implant was then removed, the brain returned to the STS solution for one hour.

steps.title: Once the brains sank, coronal sections...
steps.instruction: Once the brains sank, coronal sections (50 µm) were sliced with a cryostat (Leica CM3050 S) and transferred to PBS for storage.

steps.title: Treated sections were then incubated for...
steps.instruction: Treated sections were then incubated for 24-48 hours at 4°C in 5% NGS, 0.2% triton, and a GFP primary antibody (1:1000) (see Materials ).

steps.title: Slices were then washed, and incubated...
steps.instruction: Slices were then washed, and incubated for 2 hours at room temperature in 5% NGS, 0.2% PBST, and an Alexa™ 488 secondary antibody (1:200) (see Materials ).

steps.title: Finally, stained sections were mounted onto...
steps.instruction: Finally, stained sections were mounted onto glass slides using Vectashield Mounting Medium (see Materials ).

steps.title: Confocal images were acquired with an Olympus VS120 slide scanner
steps.instruction: Confocal images were acquired with an Olympus VS120 slide scanner.

steps.title: The brains were then moved to...
steps.instruction: The brains were then moved to a solution of 30-40% sucrose in PBS.

steps.title: Sections were then blocked and treated...
steps.instruction: Sections were then blocked and treated in a 5% normal goat serum (NGS) and 0.2% PBS Triton solution (PBST).
